scope.summary: Extend the glTF morph-target export (MT-249) and the Alembic workaround path (MT-252) to carry blend-shape/morph channel animation weights as time-sampled data, matching MD 2026.0 morph animation capability
acceptance_criteria: The glTF morph export (MT-249 / gltf_morph.rs) writes a glTF animation object with weight tracks per morph target, sampled at the export fps, so the blend-shape weights animate correctly in Blender and Three.js
The animation weight track uses STEP or LINEAR interpolation mode (from the Track<f32> system, MT-282) rather than always being step-only
The Blender-Python Alembic conversion recipe (proposed in the Alembic import MT above) is extended to also bake morph-channel weights into the Alembic ICurves so Houdini and Maya can read them
An animation export range parameter (start_frame, end_frame) is respected in the weight track output, matching MD 2026.0 Animation Range Export feature
scope.risk_if_missed: Without morph-weight animation in the export, blend-shape driven cloth deformation (avatar body-shape animation driving garment re-fit) cannot be communicated to downstream DCC tools, reducing Tailor's animation export to static cloth sim only.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
